title: TokenCache for Hadoop MapReduce Security
description: The TokenCache.java file in Hadoop MapReduce's security directory is responsible for managing and transferring security tokens, such as delegation tokens, between job clients and tasks. It provides methods for obtaining tokens for namenodes, cleaning up token references, merging binary tokens, loading tokens from a file, storing job tokens, and retrieving shuffle and encrypted spill keys. This class plays a crucial role in maintaining secure communication within the MapReduce framework, ensuring that tasks have the necessary credentials to interact with the Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) and other cluster components. It collaborates with various Hadoop classes and interfaces, including Configuration, FileSystem, Path, JobConf, and Credentials, to achieve its functionality. The class also has dependencies on external libraries like SLF4J for logging and Apache Commons Lang for utility functions.

It provides methods for obtaining tokens for namenodes, cleaning up token references, merging binary tokens, loading tokens from a file, storing job tokens, and retrieving shuffle and encrypted spill keys. This class plays a crucial role in maintaining secure communication within the MapReduce framework, ensuring that tasks have the necessary credentials to interact with the Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) and other cluster components. It collaborates with various Hadoop classes and interfaces, including Configuration, FileSystem, Path, JobConf, and Credentials, to achieve its functionality.
